title: Neomammillaria neoformosa
intro: Neomammillaria neoformosa, also known as the "Mammillaria neoformosa," is a species of cactus that belongs to the family Cactaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which may help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Neomammillaria neoformosa include glycosides, flavonoids, and saponins, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tincture, infusion, or powder, and is used to treat various conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Neomammillaria neoformosa is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the genus Neomammillaria. Neomammillaria neoformosa is often classified within the subfamily Cactoideae. This species is further categorized within the tribe Cacteae. The classification of Neomammillaria neoformosa is also sometimes adjusted according to the most recent taxonomic research.
